title: ASIC moves to extend managed investment scheme relief measures
full_text:
Editor's Choice
Sequoia Financial chair resigns
The embattled financial services firm has seen its chair step down as it continues to deal with the fallout of the Shield and First Guardian collapses.
'No basis' to ban independent trustee model: Equity Trustees
Equity Trustees has made its submission to Treasury for enhancing member protections saying the blame for the Shield and First Guardian collapses rests with the responsible entities.
Old settings for TPD 'no longer fit': APRA
APRA executive director of superannuation, life and private health insurance Jane Magill said total and permanent disability insurance (TPD) in its current form is being tested by the sharp rise in mental health claims.
More advisers go self-licensed to avoid associative risks
An industry expert said financial advisers are increasingly moving towards a self-licensed model to gain better control of their business, while avoiding the risks of operating under large dealer groups.
Products
Featured Profile
Matt Lawler
CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER
AKUMIN FINANCIAL PLANNING PTY LIMITED
AKUMIN FINANCIAL PLANNING PTY LIMITED
Akumin chief executive Matt Lawler is steering AMP's adviser network through its split from the wealth giant and charting a new path forward. Riddhima Talwani writes.
author: Vinny Vucago
heading: Regulation
page_title: ASIC moves to extend managed investment scheme relief measures | Financial Standard
